steps.title: Identify the type of thought experiment
steps.content: The key phrase is “would have gone if the ball that altered its path did not exist.” This describes imagining an alternative reality: what would have happened under different circumstances than what actually occurred.

steps.title: Select the term for this reasoning
steps.content: Counterfactual thinking is reasoning about what would have happened if actual events had been different—exactly this type of mental simulation. Ambivalent (conflicting feelings), analogical (reasoning by comparison), and associative (linking concepts) describe different cognitive processes entirely. That matches choice B — B is correct.
